question.label: Early decisions
question.name: When did you last miss the chance to shape a decision early?
question.description: Anchors her self-read in a specific recent example of late influence.

answer: Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.

assessment.note: She did not name a specific missed moment; instead she surfaced recurring feedback about being unclear with non-technical partners, a strong clarity gap. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'non-technical partners' feedback could be drilled next session, but drill cap forces stage advance.

new_queue.label: Impact in practice
new_queue.name: What changed because of your work that would not have happened otherwise?

new_queue.label: Signal to stakeholders
new_queue.name: How did you make your work visible to the people who needed to see it?

new_queue.label: Gap awareness
new_queue.name: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?

new_queue.label: Visibility gap
new_queue.name: What part of your contribution is easiest for others to miss?

new_queue.label: Leverage choices
new_queue.name: Where do you choose to go deep instead of pulling others in earlier?

new_queue.label: Next-step commitment
new_queue.name: What is one change you will make in the next two weeks to raise your visibility?

history.answer_excerpt: I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I get work done, yet I am not consistently shaping decisions early.
Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.
